execution_rules.summary: The full MVP now runs. The next objective is to make the single-crate Rust CLI repeatable and controllable before any broad module split.
rules: Keep the implementation in the single app-cli crate until the execution contract is stable.
The MVP must run through the single-crate Rust CLI so worker agents share one command surface and cannot fork ad hoc script behavior.
The active lesson target is L2 unless a missing external dependency makes L1 the only runnable local fallback.
The pipeline must produce plain text, guided story JSON, question bank JSON, and textbook MDX in the existing research/pipeline layout.
Every model call must save the rendered prompt and raw response so failures can be debugged without rerunning previous steps blindly.
Minimum validation is required: generated JSON must parse, guided story manifest must reference existing step files, and textbook question references must point at generated question IDs or families.
Asset-level relevance scoring must write sidecar reports under research/pipeline/meta and must not mutate generated learning assets.
Implement execution control before broad module decomposition: stage selection, skip existing outputs, force rerun, and clear run summaries come before new crates.
Defer provider abstraction, typed model crates, and reviewer gates until the repeatable single-crate runner is stable.

milestones.name: Full Course Generation MVP Runs
done_when: One command or documented command sequence runs a lesson from raw PDF/plain text to guided story, question bank, and textbook outputs.
The resulting files exist in research/pipeline/1-plain, research/pipeline/3-guided_story/<lesson_id>/stepN, and research/pipeline/5-textbook.
The generated JSON files parse and the generated MDX is saved for inspection.

milestones.name: Step-Local Asset Layout
done_when: Guided story output is lesson-local under research/pipeline/3-guided_story/<lesson_id>.
Each step owns its own step.json and question_bank.json.
No lesson-level question bank file is treated as the source of truth.

milestones.name: Repeatable Single-Crate Runner
done_when: `coursegen run L2` can skip existing successful outputs by default.
A developer can force selected stages to rerun without deleting files by hand.
Each run writes a concise summary explaining which stages were reused, generated, failed, or validated.

milestones.name: Programmatic Quality Gates
done_when: Generated guided story, question bank, and textbook outputs pass structural and cross-reference quality gates.
Coverage trace, linked steps, question ids, family ids, term ids, and MDX component references are checked without relying on reviewer prose.
Validation reports explain exactly what failed and which file/path caused it.

milestones.name: Local Course Artifact Viewers
done_when: A local Vite React app can preview generated textbook MDX.
A local Vite React app can preview guided story steps and their step-local question banks.
The viewer reads existing research/pipeline artifacts without creating a second course source of truth.

milestones.name: Reusable Exam Signal Cache
done_when: Exam PDFs under research/pipeline/0-raw/exam can be converted through the Rust CLI.
Converted exam text is cached under research/pipeline/1-plain/exam/<pdf_stem>/plain.txt.
Relevance scoring reads cached exam PDF text instead of treating PDF exams as unavailable.

phases.name: Run the Full MVP
phases.description: Build the shortest reliable path that turns one real lesson into all downstream course assets before resuming architecture work.
phases.objective: Make the full course-generation workflow run end to end once, with inspectable artifacts and minimal validation.
entry_criteria: The repository contains at least one raw PDF under research/pipeline/0-raw.
Existing prompts for guided story, question bank, and textbook are available under research/prompts.
MinerU and LLM credentials can be supplied through local environment variables or token files when the real APIs are used.
exit_criteria: A developer can run the MVP without reading the old roadmap.
The flow creates or reuses plain text for the lesson.
The flow generates guided story manifest and step JSON files.
The flow generates a question bank JSON file.
The flow generates a textbook MDX file.
A minimal validation command reports pass/fail for parseability and core references.
deliverables: Rendered prompt and raw response audit files
parallel_tracks: No parallel roadmap tracks are active before MVP completion.
gate_checks: No new crate or abstraction is added unless it directly reduces the time to a runnable Rust MVP.
No task is split out for resume, retry, reviewer gates, or module decomposition before the MVP output exists.
The MVP is judged by generated files and validation results, not by framework completeness.

tasks.title: Run one lesson through the full Rust MVP pipeline
tasks.description: Implement the shortest Rust CLI path that creates all MVP course artifacts for one lesson before investing in resume, retry, review gates, or module decomposition.
done_when: `coursegen run <lesson_id>` accepts a lesson id and target language option.
If research/pipeline/1-plain/<lesson_id>/plain.txt is missing, `coursegen run` can create it through the existing Rust convert path or fail with a clear credential/setup error.
Guided story output is written to research/pipeline/3-guided_story/<lesson_id> with a manifest and step-local step files.
Question bank output is written beside its owning step at research/pipeline/3-guided_story/<lesson_id>/stepN/question_bank.json.
Textbook output is written to research/pipeline/5-textbook/<lesson_id>.mdx.
Rendered prompts and raw model responses are saved under research/pipeline/meta/<lesson_id>/mvp.
A minimal validator confirms JSON parseability and basic cross-file references.

phases.name: Move Assets to Step-Local Layout
phases.description: Keep the single-crate Rust CLI, but move guided story and question bank outputs into a lesson/step-local directory layout with one source of truth.
phases.objective: Make each step self-contained by storing `step.json` and its only question bank beside each other under `research/pipeline/3-guided_story/<lesson_id>/stepN/`.
entry_criteria: P0-T01 completed with real L2 outputs.
L2 plain text, guided story, question bank, textbook, and audit files exist in the old MVP layout.
The app still uses one Rust crate: crates/app-cli.
exit_criteria: Manifest lives at `research/pipeline/3-guided_story/<lesson_id>/manifest.json`.
Each guided story step lives at `research/pipeline/3-guided_story/<lesson_id>/stepN/step.json`.
Each step question bank lives at `research/pipeline/3-guided_story/<lesson_id>/stepN/question_bank.json`.
Questions are owned by exactly one step; cross-step linked questions are rejected.
Textbook generation and validation collect question banks by scanning step directories.
`research/pipeline/4-question_bank` is not used as the source of truth.
deliverables: Validator support for step-local question banks
parallel_tracks: No parallel tracks; this is a narrow execution-control pass.
gate_checks: No new crate is introduced.
No new crate is introduced.
Do not create a lesson-level question bank index as a second source of truth.
Do not allow question families or variants to link to multiple steps.
Keep this as a path/layout change; do not introduce multi-step planning in the same task.

tasks.title: Restructure guided story and question bank into step-local assets
tasks.description: Move guided story and question bank outputs into `research/pipeline/3-guided_story/<lesson_id>/stepN/`, keeping each question bank owned by exactly one step.
done_when: `coursegen run <lesson_id>` writes `research/pipeline/3-guided_story/<lesson_id>/manifest.json`.
`coursegen run <lesson_id>` writes `research/pipeline/3-guided_story/<lesson_id>/stepN/step.json` for every generated guided story step.
`coursegen run <lesson_id>` writes `research/pipeline/3-guided_story/<lesson_id>/stepN/question_bank.json` for every generated guided story step.
`coursegen validate <lesson_id>` scans step directories for question banks and rejects any question family or variant with linked_steps outside its owning step.
Textbook generation reads all step-local question banks instead of `research/pipeline/4-question_bank/<lesson_id>.question_bank.json`.
CLI tests assert the new paths and step-local question bank validation.

phases.name: Make the MVP Runner Repeatable
phases.description: Keep the single-crate Rust CLI, but add enough execution control that development does not waste API quota or require manual file cleanup.
phases.objective: Make `coursegen run` practical for day-to-day development by reusing existing artifacts, allowing targeted reruns, and writing clear run summaries.
entry_criteria: P1-T01 completed with step-local guided story and question bank outputs.
L2 plain text, guided story, step-local question bank, textbook, and audit files exist.
The app still uses one Rust crate: crates/app-cli.
exit_criteria: `coursegen run L2` reuses existing valid stage outputs by default.
`coursegen run L2 --force-stage <stage>` can rerun a selected stage and downstream dependent stages.
`coursegen run L2 --from-stage <stage>` can start at a selected stage when upstream artifacts already validate.
The CLI prints and writes a run summary with stage status, output paths, and audit paths.
Tests cover skip, force, from-stage, and validation failure behavior without real network calls.
parallel_tracks: No parallel tracks; this is a narrow execution-control pass.
gate_checks: No new crate is introduced.
No provider abstraction or reviewer framework is introduced.
A failed downstream stage must not force MinerU or earlier LLM stages to rerun unless explicitly requested.
Existing generated outputs are never overwritten unless the user passes an explicit force flag.

tasks.title: Add asset-level relevance scoring sidecar
tasks.description: Add a single-crate CLI command that scores step, question-family, textbook-section, and coverage relevance from step-local assets plus weak exam signals, writing a sidecar report without changing generated course assets.
done_when: `coursegen score-relevance <lesson_id> --target-language zh-CN` reads `research/pipeline/3-guided_story/<lesson_id>/manifest.json` and every step-local `stepN/step.json` plus `stepN/question_bank.json`.
The scorer reads `research/pipeline/1-plain/<lesson_id>/plain.txt`, `research/pipeline/2-related_important/course_desc.md` when present, and `research/pipeline/5-textbook/<lesson_id>.mdx`.
Exam files under `research/pipeline/0-raw/exam` are used only as weak scoring signal; `.txt` and `.md` files are included, while PDFs are represented by filename and `exam_pdf_text_unavailable`.
Rendered prompts, request JSON, raw response, extracted content, and final report are written under `research/pipeline/meta/<lesson_id>/relevance/`.
`research/pipeline/meta/<lesson_id>/relevance/report.json` contains step_scores, question_family_scores, textbook_section_scores, and coverage_scores arrays.
Scorer output validation rejects invalid JSON, unknown step sequence ids, and unknown step-local question family ids.
CLI smoke tests cover local LLM stub success and clear missing key/model failures without real network calls.

tasks.title: Add stage reuse and targeted rerun controls
tasks.description: Teach the single-crate CLI to treat the MVP stages as reusable artifacts, so developers can rerun only the stage they need without wasting MinerU or LLM quota.
done_when: `coursegen run <lesson_id>` skips valid existing plain text, guided story, step-local question bank, and textbook outputs.
`--force-stage convert|guided-story|question-bank|textbook|all` reruns the selected stage and any downstream stages that depend on it.
`--from-stage convert|guided-story|question-bank|textbook` starts from the selected stage after validating required upstream artifacts.
Each run writes `research/pipeline/meta/<lesson_id>/runs/<run_id>/summary.json`.
The summary records stage status as reused, generated, skipped, failed, or validated.
CLI tests cover reuse, force-stage, from-stage, and a broken-reference validation failure.

tasks.title: Generate manifest-driven multi-step lessons
tasks.description: Remove the single-step MVP bottleneck by having guided story generation produce multiple natural lesson steps, then generate a step-local question bank for each manifest step.
done_when: Guided story prompts ask for a lesson-level object with multiple natural steps instead of one full-lesson MVP slice.
`coursegen run <lesson_id>` writes manifest-driven `stepN/step.json` files for every generated step.
`coursegen run <lesson_id>` loops over manifest steps and writes one `stepN/question_bank.json` per step.
Question-bank prompts require all generated linked_steps to point only to the owning step.
Question-bank model calls are audited under `research/pipeline/meta/<lesson_id>/mvp/question_bank/<step_id>/` for multi-step runs.
Transient LLM request failures are retried before failing the full run.
CLI smoke tests cover a multi-step guided story response and verify every step receives a question bank.
L1 and L2 have been regenerated into multi-step, step-local course assets and both validate.

tasks.title: Make default runs resumable
tasks.description: Teach `coursegen run` to reuse already-valid generated artifacts by default so an interrupted multi-step run can continue from missing step-local outputs instead of regenerating successful work.
done_when: `coursegen run <lesson_id>` reuses existing plain text when present.
`coursegen run <lesson_id>` reuses an existing guided story manifest and step files when they parse.
`coursegen run <lesson_id>` skips each existing parseable `stepN/question_bank.json` independently and only generates missing or forced step question banks.
If any question bank is generated during a resumed run, textbook generation reruns so references stay consistent.
A fully generated lesson can run without LLM credentials because no model calls are needed.
`--force-stage convert|guided-story|question-bank|textbook|all` can explicitly invalidate reuse for the selected stage and downstream stages.
CLI smoke tests cover resuming from a missing step question bank without regenerating guided story or existing step question banks.

phases.name: Add Programmatic Quality Gates
phases.description: Keep the single-crate runner, but raise validation from basic parseability to practical content safety checks before adding reviewer loops or crate boundaries.
phases.objective: Catch broken generated course assets through deterministic validation: coverage, references, term catalogs, MDX component references, and audit completeness.
entry_criteria: P2-T01 completed with repeatable stage reuse and run summaries.
L2 generated outputs and audit files are available as real validation samples.
The app still uses one Rust crate: crates/app-cli.
exit_criteria: `coursegen validate L2` emits a structured validation report.
Guided story manifest and step files are cross-checked for sequence ids, files, term ids, screen ids, and required fields.
Question bank validation checks family ids, question ids, variants, linked_steps, coverage_map, and duplicate ids.
Textbook validation checks sectionMap, coverageTrace, QuestionRef, QuestionFamily, Term, Formula, and Figure references.
Validation failures identify the artifact path and logical field path.
Tests cover at least one failure case per generated asset family.
parallel_tracks: No parallel tracks; keep this as a narrow validator pass.
gate_checks: No new crate is introduced.
No LLM reviewer is introduced in this phase.
Validation must be deterministic and runnable offline.
Validation errors must be actionable enough for a worker agent to fix the generated artifact or prompt.

tasks.title: Add deterministic quality gates for generated assets
tasks.description: Expand the single-crate validator so generated guided story, question bank, and textbook assets fail fast on broken references, missing coverage, malformed catalogs, or unusable MDX component links.
done_when: `coursegen validate <lesson_id>` writes `research/pipeline/meta/<lesson_id>/validation/report.json`.
Guided story validation checks manifest step files, sequence ids, screen ids, term_catalog entries, introduced_terms, focus_terms, exercises, and media paths.
Question bank validation checks coverage_map references, family ids, question ids, linked_steps, variant counts, answer fields, and duplicate ids.
Textbook validation checks frontmatter sectionMap, coverageTrace, section ids, QuestionRef ids, QuestionFamily ids, Term ids, Formula tags, and Figure paths.
Validation output contains severity, artifact path, logical path, and message for each finding.
Tests include broken guided story manifest, missing question family, bad coverage map, and broken textbook reference fixtures.

phases.name: Build Local Artifact Viewers
phases.description: Add a React + Tailwind + Vite + Jotai viewer app for inspecting generated MDX textbooks, guided story steps, and learning practice assets from the existing pipeline layout.
phases.objective: Make generated lessons inspectable in a browser without adding a backend or creating a duplicate data model.
entry_criteria: L2 generated outputs exist under research/pipeline/3-guided_story/L2 and research/pipeline/5-textbook/L2.mdx.
Step-local question banks exist beside their owning step files.
exit_criteria: `npm install` and `npm run dev -- --host 127.0.0.1` work from apps/viewer.
The MDX viewer renders the selected lesson textbook with custom placeholders for generated course components.
The storybook viewer renders the selected lesson manifest, step screens, step-local question families, and relevance treatments.
The practice viewer separates active-recall flashcards from assessment-like quiz items and longform questions.
The viewer navigation supports course creators reviewing many lectures without treating asset type tabs as the course structure.
The app uses Jotai for selected lesson/view/step state.
The app uses Tailwind for layout and styling.
The viewer reads pipeline artifacts through Vite-served static files or dev-only fetches and does not rewrite generated artifacts.
parallel_tracks: Worker implements the viewer app while the architect reviews task boundaries and validates the finished app.
gate_checks: Do not add a backend service in this pass.
Do not move or duplicate research/pipeline artifacts as a new source of truth.
Keep the UI focused on inspection and debugging, not learner-facing polish.
Do not change Rust CLI behavior for this task.

tasks.title: Add React artifact viewer app
tasks.description: Create apps/viewer with React, Tailwind, Vite, and Jotai to inspect generated MDX textbooks and guided story step-local assets.
done_when: The app lists available lessons from a small manifest and defaults to L2 when available.
MDX viewer renders research/pipeline/5-textbook/<lesson_id>.mdx with readable typography and safe placeholder components for QuestionFamily, QuestionRef, Term, Formula, and Figure.
Storybook viewer renders research/pipeline/3-guided_story/<lesson_id>/manifest.json and stepN/step.json files with screen navigation.
Each step view shows its colocated stepN/question_bank.json families and variants.
When present, research/pipeline/meta/<lesson_id>/relevance/report.json is shown as step/question/textbook relevance badges.
Jotai owns selected lesson, selected viewer surface, and selected step state.
`npm run build` succeeds.

tasks.title: Separate creator practice surfaces
tasks.description: Update prompts and the viewer so flashcards are active-recall spaced-repetition assets, quiz items are assessment-like assets, and course creators can review lectures through a workspace layout rather than a flat tab strip.
done_when: Question-bank prompts explicitly forbid single-choice and true-false items inside flashcard families.
Question-bank prompts add quiz_families for assessment-like short questions.
Textbook prompts distinguish flashcards, quiz items, and longform questions when embedding practice.
Relevance scoring includes quiz_families when scoring question-family relevance.
The viewer presents a course-creator workspace with lecture selection separate from asset review surfaces.
The practice viewer renders Flashcards, Quiz, and Longform as separate groups.
`npm run build` succeeds for apps/viewer and Rust tests still pass.

phases.name: Cache Exam PDF Signals
phases.description: Convert public exam PDFs into reusable plain-text signals and feed them into asset-level relevance scoring without creating another source of truth.
phases.objective: Make exam PDF parsing reusable across relevance scoring runs so scarce exam evidence can guide filtering without repeated MinerU calls.
entry_criteria: MinerU credentials can be supplied through local environment variables or token files.
exit_criteria: `coursegen convert-exams` converts all raw exam PDFs by default while skipping already cached plain text.
`coursegen convert-exams --force` can intentionally regenerate exam PDF caches.
Exam cache output lives under research/pipeline/1-plain/exam/<pdf_stem>/ with plain.txt, job.json, result.zip, and artifacts.
Relevance scorer input includes cached PDF plain text and cache paths.
CLI smoke tests cover cached exam PDF text flowing into the relevance prompt.
parallel_tracks: No parallel tracks; this is a narrow pipeline reuse pass.
gate_checks: Do not create a second exam source of truth outside research/pipeline/1-plain/exam.
Do not rerun MinerU when plain.txt already exists unless forced.
Keep the implementation in crates/app-cli.

tasks.title: Convert and reuse exam PDF text
tasks.description: Add a Rust CLI command that parses exam PDFs into a reusable plain-text cache, then teach relevance scoring to include cached exam PDF content.
done_when: `coursegen convert-exams` scans research/pipeline/0-raw/exam for PDFs.
The command writes each PDF cache to research/pipeline/1-plain/exam/<pdf_stem>/plain.txt.
The command skips existing plain.txt caches by default and reports converted=0 on a fully cached rerun.
Relevance scoring includes cached PDF content with note exam_pdf_text_cached.
Both current raw exam PDFs have been parsed into the cache layout.
